description: The controller operates as either a Root Complex (RC) or an End Point (EP) using a dual-mode core. Mode selection is done via bootstrap pins PCIESSMODE[1:0] and can be overridden in software. In RC mode it exposes Type 1 configuration space and supports downstream configuration, memory, and I/O transactions. In EP mode it supports legacy or native endpoint behaviors with BAR-mapped application space. The module supports a single bidirectional link with automatic lane reversal and dynamic width conversion.
dependencies: Correct SerDes configuration and link training; DEVSTAT.PCIESSMODE setting
Remarks: Select RC or EP prior to link enable; ensure software sequences per Section 2.11 before enumeration.

description: The PHY and core support PCIe Gen1 at 2.5 GT/s and Gen2 at 5.0 GT/s. Link width is configurable up to x2 lanes with automatic lane reversal. Gen2 speed change is directed with PL_GEN2.DIR_SPD after initial Gen1 link up. Throughput uses 8b/10b encoding and the link carries packets with address and variable payload. The design provides ultra-low TX/RX latency per the feature list.
dependencies: Stable reference clock; SerDes PLL lock; LTSSM enabled
Remarks: Set LINK_MODE/LN_EN for desired width and use PL_GEN2.DIR_SPD to switch to Gen2 as needed.

description: The module exposes one 128-bit VBUSM master port and one 128-bit VBUSM slave port to the SoC interconnect. The master port sources inbound PCIe requests into the system, and the slave port accepts outbound requests and provides MMR access. Both ports are available in RC and EP modes. Clocks for VBUSM come from the main PLL and require no per-module configuration. This interface is the path for DMA and CPU-initiated transfers to or from PCIe.
dependencies: SoC interconnect and clocking present
Remarks: Size transfers per internal bus alignment rules; map PCIe address spaces appropriately.

description: Outbound TLPs can be translated using 32 equally sized regions, each 1, 2, 4, or 8 MB. The OB_SIZE register selects region size; OB_OFFSET_INDEXn/OB_OFFSETn_HI program 32/64-bit PCIe base addresses per region. Indexing is extracted from the internal address according to the region size. Transactions crossing a translation boundary have unspecified behavior and must be sized accordingly. 64-bit addressing is supported via OB_OFFSETn_HI.
dependencies: CMD_STATUS.OB_XLT_EN=1; Proper OB_SIZE and offsets programmed
Remarks: Avoid transfers that cross region limits; align buffers to region size for large bursts.

feature_name: Inbound address translation (4 regions, BAR matching)
description: Inbound accesses are mapped to internal address space using four regions associated with BARs. IB_BARn selects the BAR match; IB_STARTn_HI/LO define start address and IB_OFFSETn defines internal base. BAR0 is reserved to application registers for both RC and EP and cannot be remapped. BAR1 may optionally serve as translation start when its region start is left zero. 32-bit and 64-bit BAR concatenation is supported as described.
dependencies: BAR programming; CMD_STATUS.IB_XLT_EN=1
Remarks: Size and place windows to avoid overlap; use BAR1-as-start only when no other inbound regions are needed.

feature_name: Configuration space Type 0/1 and capability chains
description: The controller exposes standard PCIe configuration spaces with Type 0 for EP and Type 1 for RC. Capabilities are linked via CAP_PTR in Type 0/1 and include Power Management, MSI, PCIe Capabilities, and Extended Capabilities. Software can pre-initialize read-only fields and BAR masks via DBI_CS2 before enumeration. Capability next pointers are writable from the internal bus for chaining as required. Layout follows the offsets summarized in Table 2-7.
dependencies: Correct mode selection; internal bus access prior to enumeration
Remarks: Clear DBI_CS2 after programming; do not modify from the serial link side.

feature_name: BAR Mask overlay and sizing control
description: BAR mask registers overlay the BARs to let software control requested size, type, and enable prior to RC enumeration. DBI_CS2 must be set to write mask values; reads return BAR contents, not mask. BAR0 mask is restricted (only bits [31:20] configurable) because BAR0 maps to application registers. Programming should be done only via internal bus; serial-side modification is unsupported. Examples show verifying masks by writing test patterns and reading back masked BARs.
dependencies: DBI_CS2 gating; internal bus access path
Remarks: Write masks then clear DBI_CS2; validate by test writes as recommended in the user guide.

description: The subsystem supports both little- and big-endian system operation even though PCIe is little-endian on the wire. ENDIAN register programs swap granularity (1/2/4/8-byte) for inbound and outbound paths. Detailed tables define byte-lane remapping for both directions to maintain data ordering. MMR and non-prefetchable memory over 64-bit bus use 32-bit aligned swapping (Mode C) as noted. Proper configuration ensures compatibility with big-endian cores.
dependencies: System endianness; correct ENDIAN_MODE setting
Remarks: Confirm strobe/byte-enable behavior with the selected swap mode, especially for 64-bit memory paths.

feature_name: Transaction alignment, burst, and byte-strobe rules
description: Transactions on the internal bus must be ≤128 bytes and aligned to avoid boundary splitting. Writes must have a single contiguous run of asserted byte enables; holes are not allowed. Misaligned outbound writes must be limited to 120 bytes; inbound reads crossing 128-byte boundaries may be split. Only incremental bursts are supported; fixed or wrap bursts lead to undefined behavior. Observing these rules prevents bus lockup and preserves throughput.
dependencies: Adherence to bus master and EDMA configuration limits
Remarks: Program EDMA descriptors to 128-byte (or smaller) incremental bursts with contiguous strobes.

description: The controller supports a maximum outbound payload of 128 bytes. Inbound maximum payload size is 256 bytes. The maximum remote read request size is 256 bytes. These limits interact with interleaving behavior where read responses are not interleaved if reads do not exceed the core’s max transaction size (128 bytes). Sizing payloads accordingly optimizes flow control and latency.
dependencies: RC/EP negotiated MPS/MRRS; DMA configuration
Remarks: Match host MPS/MRRS; prefer ≤128-byte read requests to avoid response interleaving.

description: The PCIe module has no internal DMA engine. System DMA (e.g., EDMA) should drive the slave port for outbound writes/reads and the master port services inbound requests. Example EDMA configurations are provided for memory write and read using payload-sized aCnt and total buffer size in bCnt. Expected sustainable bandwidth is ~85% of 2.0 Gbps (Gen1) or 4.0 Gbps (Gen2) per lane. Software must schedule DMA respecting PCIe and internal bus rules.
dependencies: Available SoC DMA; configured address translations
Remarks: Allocate buffers aligned to translation regions and 128-byte boundaries to meet burst and strobe requirements.

description: EPs can assert/deassert legacy INTA/B/C/D via EP_IRQ_SET/CLR which transmit INTx messages upstream. RC receives legacy interrupts and must poll downstream devices’ configuration to identify the source. Each legacy line has raw/status/enable registers and an EOI register acknowledges events. Interrupt delivery timing is not deterministic due to message transport. Software must handle deassert races relative to EOI.
dependencies: Link operational; INTx enabled in config space
Remarks: Read downstream status before issuing EOI to avoid retrigger; only one EP legacy interrupt can be pending at a time.

description: The module supports MSI with up to 32 vectors mapped into grouped raw/status/enable registers. EP generates MSI by issuing a memory write to the MSI address with MSI data (vector) configured by RC. RC receives MSIs into MSI_IRQ, with status bits requiring write-1-to-clear and IRQ_EOI signaling. The design also allows MSI-like writes from RC to EP or EP-to-EP using BAR0 MSI_IRQ in the peer. Multiple vectors share interrupt event numbers per Table 2-10.
dependencies: MSI_CAP.MSI_EN=1; MSI address/data configured
Remarks: Clear MSIn_IRQ_STATUS and write IRQ_EOI to re-arm; ensure INTx disabled when MSI is enabled.

description: A total of fourteen interrupt events are exposed including grouped MSI events, legacy lines, error summary, and PM/reset. Error events include system, fatal, non-fatal, correctable, AXI bridge fatal, and AER. Power/reset events include PME, ACK, turn-off, and link reset request; link-down and transaction error interrupts are also described. Interrupt enable/clear/status triplets are provided per group. Proper masking and EOI handling are required for reliable service.
dependencies: IRQ enables set; IRQ controller routing
Remarks: Map only required events to CPU; handle write-1-to-clear semantics and EOI ordering.

description: The PHY uses a differential reference clock that the SerDes PLL multiplies to the required line rate. Systems often use a common reference clock and may use SSC; COMMON_CLK_CFG must reflect the architecture. The PCIe subsystem is unusable until the SerDes PLL is configured and PCIE_SERDES_STS.LOCK is asserted. VBUSM clocks derive from the device main PLL and require no PCIe-local setup. Link training sequences increase when a common clock is not used.
dependencies: Valid REFCLK; SerDes PLL configured and locked
Remarks: Set COMMON_CLK_CFG appropriately and avoid transactions before LOCK=1; expect longer training without a shared clock.

feature_name: Device and Link power management (ASPM L0s/L1)
description: The controller supports device D-states D0/D1/D2/D3hot and ASPM link states L0s and L1 (no L2). Transitions may be hardware-driven (ASPM) or software-initiated via PM capability and LINK_STAT_CTRL. L1/L0s entry/exit latencies are programmable and reported in capability registers. The relationship between device and link states is documented to guide policy. PM messages can be transmitted with PMCMD and related status is latched in PMRST registers.
dependencies: ASPM enabled in config; power domains managed
Remarks: Avoid L2 usage (unsupported); program ACK_FREQ latency knobs to balance power and wake times.

feature_name: Power domain on/off and reset sequencing
description: Turning PCIe power or module state off and back on resets the module to initial state. All inbound/outbound traffic must be quiesced first or system hang may occur. After re-enabling power, software must reconfigure and restart link training. RSTCMD supports hot reset downstream with FLUSH_N used to ensure no pending transactions. Post-reset, software must delay at least 100 ms before new transactions and up to 1.5 s for remote responsiveness.
dependencies: PSC/power controller; quiesced traffic
Remarks: Follow GOSTAT handshakes in PSC and observe delays; restart training and re-enumerate if required.

description: RC bring-up requires enabling PCIESS power/clock, selecting RC mode, programming the SerDes PLL, and waiting for LOCK. Software disables LTSSM, configures PCIESS registers, then enables LTSSM to train the link. Link-up is confirmed when DEBUG0.LTSSM_STATE reaches 0x11 (L0). Enumeration follows with bus numbering, BAR assignment, and downstream configuration. DMA and device handshakes start after configuration completes.
dependencies: SerDes PLL; power domain on; RC mode selected
Remarks: Automate wait-for-LOCK and LTSSM gating; log LTSSM transitions for timing analysis.

description: EP bring-up mirrors RC steps but selects EP mode and may gate configuration with APP_RETRY_EN. With APP_RETRY_EN set, incoming configuration accesses receive retry until local programming finishes. Link training is enabled after configuration and verified by LTSSM state reaching L0. After clearing APP_RETRY_EN, RC configuration proceeds and DMA can start. BARs and inbound/outbound translators must be set before exposing memory windows.
dependencies: SerDes PLL; EP mode selected; APP_RETRY_EN policy
Remarks: Use APP_RETRY_EN to avoid premature config timeouts on slow firmware; clear it before RC accesses.

feature_name: PIPE and PHY loopback test modes
description: PIPE loopback can be used in RC or EP as loopback master or slave using PL_LINK_CTRL.LPBK_EN and retraining. PHY loopback is configured via SERDES_CFG0/1 TX/RX loopback fields and requires forcing LTSSM to POLL_ACTIVE. Both methods are for symbol-level validation and not for TLP echoing except PHY loopback in RC. Additional steps disable RX_LOS and set link state forcing for reliable entry. These modes assist in SerDes and link bring-up diagnostics.
dependencies: SerDes configured; LTSSM control; proper lane configuration
Remarks: Enter loopback before any traffic to avoid sequence mismatch; respect limitations on configuration TLPs in loopback.

description: DEBUG0/DEBUG1 provide visibility into transmit symbols, electrical idle, training sequences, and LTSSM state. Appendix A maps LTSSM encoded values (e.g., 0x11 = L0) for software interpretation. Additional bits expose receiver polarity reversal tests and link-up, skip reception, and compliance requests. These registers help trace training issues and runtime link state changes. The module also exposes PIPE signal reflections for deeper PHY diagnostics.
dependencies: None beyond link and clock present
Remarks: Poll during bring-up and fault conditions; record transitions to correlate with training parameters.

description: Software can inject a single ECRC or LCRC error on the next packet using DIAG_CTRL. Setting INV_ECRC or INV_LCRC flips the least significant CRC bit and auto-clears after one TLP. This aids validation of error reporting paths and AER handling. Use only in controlled test scenarios to avoid disrupting normal traffic. Combined with AER status, it provides end-to-end verification of error paths.
dependencies: Active link; traffic generator available
Remarks: Ensure system handlers for error messages are enabled before injecting for observability.

feature_name: Advanced Error Reporting (AER) and baseline error handling
description: Baseline PCI-compatible error signaling is mapped to STATUS_COMMAND and DEV_STAT_CTRL bits (e.g., SERR_EN, PAR_ERR_RESP). Extended AER provides per-error status, masking, and severity control plus header logging of first error TLP. Root ports have ROOT_ERR_CMD/ROOT_ERR_ST and ERR_SRC_ID for reception and routing of error classes. ECRC generation/checking is controllable via PCIE_ACCR and should be disabled if peers do not support it. These facilities enable robust detection, classification, and reporting of correctable and uncorrectable errors.
dependencies: AER enabled; masks and severities configured; ECRC policy set
Remarks: Set severities to system policy; disable ECRC when peers lack support (note in Section 2.16.2.3.1).

feature_name: Application register access over PCIe and locally
description: Application-specific PCIESS registers are mapped locally and into PCIe BAR0 for both RC and EP. RC can also access an EP’s application registers via the upper 1 KB of the EP’s configuration space. Accesses must be 32-bit and not burst; these windows are marked non-cacheable. This mapping allows remote control in absence of local EP software. BAR0 cannot be disabled from reaching application registers.
dependencies: Correct BAR0 mapping; RC software present
Remarks: Treat these regions as MMIO; avoid bursts and cacheable mappings to prevent protocol violations.

feature_name: Remote configuration and I/O windows (RC mode)
description: RC exposes windows for remote configuration (Type 0/1) and a 4 KB I/O access window. CFG_SETUP sets bus/device/function and type for outbound configuration cycles. IOBASE programs the base of I/O TLPs for the 4 KB window. Accesses should only be made when the link is operational; otherwise, no response may occur. These mechanisms let RC perform enumeration and legacy I/O to downstream devices.
dependencies: Active link; proper bus numbering
Remarks: Implement link-up checks before touching remote windows to avoid hangs.

feature_name: Symbol, ACK/NAK, and training timing controls
description: Programmable knobs exist for Ack frequency, NFTS/COMM_NFTS, L0s/L1 entry latency, SKP spacing, and replay/latency timers. ACK_FREQ allows acknowledging every N TLPs; SYM_NUM adjusts TS counts and timers; SYMTIMER_FLTMASK controls SKP intervals. These settings allow tuning for interoperability and power-performance trade-offs. Defaults match the PCIe spec but can be adapted for system needs. Misconfiguration can impact link stability and training time.
dependencies: Understanding of link partner behavior and clocking
Remarks: Change cautiously and validate with DEBUG registers; retain defaults unless specific issues need tuning.

feature_name: Posted/non-posted write control for internal master
description: The internal bus master’s posted write behavior is controllable, though on KeyStone devices POSTED_WR_EN is documented as not applicable. By default, internal writes are non-posted for ordering predictability. Read and write translators convert between PCIe and internal bus semantics. Application software should presume non-posted internal writes and rely on completions for ordering. This aids debug and coherency with DMA operations.
dependencies: N/A on KeyStone devices
Remarks: Keep default non-posted behavior to simplify software ordering guarantees.

description: The PRIORITY register allows marking inbound master transactions as supervisor (MST_PRIV) and assigning a PRIVID. Priority levels 0–7 can be set per inbound transaction for internal arbitration. This provides a basic privilege gating to restrict register access to supervisor mode when required. It also enables traffic prioritization on the internal bus. Use with system security policy for isolation between masters.
dependencies: System privilege model enforcement
Remarks: Set MST_PRIV=1 for sensitive register accesses; allocate PRIVID values consistently system-wide.

feature_name: Address Spaces and BAR usage policy
description: The module defines Address Space 0 (16 KB) for application/local/remote config and remote I/O, and Address Space 1 for data transfer. In EP mode, BAR0 maps to Address Space 0 and BAR1–BAR5 map to Address Space 1 in 32-bit; 64-bit uses adjacent BAR pairs. In RC mode, BAR usage is limited and filtered against Base/Limit windows to prevent misrouted TLPs. TLPs inside downstream Base/Limit ranges are rejected by RC filters and not delivered to the RC BARs. This policy ensures only intended traffic reaches local memory.
dependencies: Correct Base/Limit programming in RC
Remarks: Keep RC BARs outside downstream Base/Limit ranges unless explicitly targeting RC memory.

description: The design intentionally omits some PCIe features to simplify integration. It does not support using x2 as two x1 links, multiple VCs or TCs, function-level reset, beacon in-band wake, hot-plug hardware, vendor messaging, inbound I/O, non-incremental bursts, auxiliary power for D3cold retention, or L2 link state. Software must not rely on these behaviors. Knowing these limits avoids misconfiguration and wasted debug effort.
dependencies: N/A
Remarks: Architect the system around supported capabilities; emulate missing features in software only when feasible.
